No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to hunger, food security, or agricultural practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to health, well-being, or disease prevention.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides advanced technical education in IoT cybersecurity, equipping Master's students with practical skills for employment in a high-demand industry, directly aligning with Target 4.4.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to water resources, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course supports a rapidly growing, high-value IoT industry by providing skills for technological upgrading and innovation, contributing to economic productivity and decent job creation in cybersecurity (Targets 8.2, 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly enhances the resilience and reliability of IoT infrastructure by teaching cybersecurity techniques, addressing threats, and promoting technological innovation in the industry (Targets 9.1, 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to reducing income, social, or political inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Securing IoT devices, as taught in the course, contributes to the resilience of urban infrastructure against cyber threats and failures, indirectly supporting sustainable and resilient cities (Target 11.b).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to sustainable consumption, production, or waste management.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers international cybersecurity regulations and techniques for combating cybercrime, directly strengthening institutions, promoting the rule of law, and building capacity against digital threats (Targets 16.3, 16.6, 16.a).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's focus on international cybersecurity regulations and standards inherently promotes knowledge sharing and cooperation on technology, aligning with strengthening global partnerships (Target 17.6).
